purpose: Route product-development requests through strategy, PRD, and system-design stages without skipping prerequisites.
selection_order: Stage 1 owns raw ideas, strategy, MVP framing, and blueprints from scratch.
Stage 2 owns PRDs, user stories, acceptance criteria, and edge cases once a blueprint is validated.
Stage 3 owns schema, API, state-flow, and infrastructure design once a PRD is validated.
If the request would skip a prerequisite stage, route to the prerequisite child first and surface the risk.

children.name: labs21-chief-architect
children.summary: Handles raw ideas, strategy, MVP definition, and from-scratch blueprints.
route_when: The user is starting from a raw idea and needs the first strategic blueprint.
The work is still Stage 1 strategy, MVP framing, or architecture from scratch.
There is no validated blueprint yet and the product direction is still being shaped.
avoid_when: A validated blueprint already exists and the remaining job is PRD writing.
A validated PRD already exists and the remaining job is schema, API, or infrastructure design.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover nested skills automatically.

children.name: labs21-prd-writer
children.summary: Turns a validated blueprint into PRDs, user stories, acceptance criteria, and edge cases.
route_when: A validated blueprint already exists and the work is turning it into a PRD.
The user needs user stories, acceptance criteria, or edge-case coverage for an already-approved direction.
The task is Stage 2 product definition rather than strategy or architecture from scratch.
avoid_when: The product direction is still raw and needs strategy or MVP framing first.
A validated PRD already exists and the work is system design, schema, or API contracts.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover nested skills automatically.

children.name: labs21-system-architect
children.summary: Turns a validated PRD into concrete schemas, APIs, state flows, and infrastructure decisions.
route_when: A validated PRD already exists and the work is system design for v1.
The user needs schema, API contract, state-flow, or infrastructure decisions.
The task is Stage 3 engineering architecture rather than product definition.
avoid_when: The product direction is still being shaped and needs strategy or MVP framing first.
There is no validated PRD yet and the work should stay in PRD definition.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover nested skills automatically.
